wc2026.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 · Full schedule, venues, groups and kick-off times
wc2026.description: FIFA World Cup 2026 (USA, Mexico, Canada): complete schedule for all 104 matches, 16 stadiums and 12 groups. When it starts and when the final is played.
keywords: when does the World Cup 2026 start
when is the World Cup 2026 final

hero.intro: 48 teams. 104 matches. 16 cities. 3 countries. The first World Cup in the new format.

groups.description: The top two plus the eight best third-placed teams qualify, advancing to a round of 32 unprecedented in World Cups. Mexico opens the tournament in Group A, Canada plays in B and the United States in D.

faq.h2: What people search most about the World Cup 2026

items.q: When does the World Cup 2026 start?
items.a: The World Cup 2026 kicks off on Thursday, June 11, 2026 with Mexico vs South Africa at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City (1:00 PM local time).

items.q: When is the World Cup 2026 final?
items.a: The World Cup 2026 final will be played on Sunday, July 19, 2026 at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey (3:00 PM ET).

items.q: Where is the World Cup 2026 played?
items.a: The World Cup 2026 is held at 16 stadiums across three countries: 11 in the United States, 3 in Mexico (Azteca, Akron and BBVA) and 2 in Canada (BMO Field in Toronto and BC Place in Vancouver).

items.q: How many teams are in the World Cup 2026?
items.a: For the first time in history, 48 teams play the final tournament, split into 12 groups of 4. Previously there were 32.

items.q: What group is Spain in at the World Cup 2026?
items.a: Spain plays in Group H along with Uruguay, Saudi Arabia and Cape Verde. Spain debuts on June 15 against Cape Verde at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta.

items.q: How many matches are played at the World Cup 2026?
items.a: 104 matches in total: 72 group-stage, 16 round of 32, 8 round of 16, 4 quarter-finals, 2 semi-finals, the third-place match and the final.

ctaBottom.dondeVer: Where to watch the World Cup 2026

wc2026.eventLdDescription: First World Cup with 48 teams, hosted by the United States, Mexico and Canada from June 11 to July 19, 2026.

dondeVer.title: Where to watch the 2026 World Cup in Spain · DAZN, Movistar Plus+ and free options
dondeVer.description: How to watch every 2026 FIFA World Cup match in Spain: Movistar Plus+ via DAZN, free-to-air options, Spain national-team fixtures, schedule and prices updated April 2026.
dondeVer.intro: How to watch all 104 matches of the 2026 World Cup in Spain: Movistar Plus+, free-to-air RTVE, DAZN and tips to follow Spain vs Cape Verde, Saudi Arabia and Uruguay without missing a minute. Updated to April 2026.
dondeVer.abroadTitle: Watching the World Cup from outside Spain
keywords: how to watch USA Mexico World Cup 2026

rtve.coverage: Opening match, all Spain matches, one match per matchday, round of 16, quarter-finals, semi-finals and final
rtve.extras: Free-to-air on La 1 and RTVE Play. Spanish-language commentary.

movistar.coverage: All 104 matches live + 4K HDR + Dolby audio
movistar.extras: Pre- and post-match analysis, exclusive content

dazn.extras: App, browser, smart TV. Multiple devices

abroad.h2: Watching the World Cup from outside Spain
abroad.intro: If you're traveling to USA / Mexico / Canada during the World Cup, here are three practical paths to not miss a match.
abroad.ueGeoportBody: If you travel to another EU country, the app works just like in Spain. Outside the EU you'll need another solution.
abroad.esimUsaBody: Holafly or Airalo give you data in the US with a Spanish IP on some plans. €30-70 for the whole trip.
abroad.localUsaBody: Fox + Tubi (free with ads) in English. Telemundo / Universo + Peacock in Spanish. Paying Peacock one month ($8) covers the entire tournament.

items.q: Who has the World Cup 2026 rights in the US?
items.a: Fox holds English-language rights and broadcasts every match on Fox/FS1 with free ad-supported access on Tubi. Telemundo / Universo carry Spanish-language broadcasts with on-demand replay on Peacock. Both options can be combined: Fox for English, Telemundo + Peacock for Spanish at $8/month.

items.q: Can the World Cup 2026 be watched for free in the US?
items.a: Yes. Fox provides free over-the-air broadcast through local affiliates plus free streaming on Tubi (ad-supported). The Spanish-language Telemundo signal is also available over the air for households with an antenna.

items.q: How much does it cost to watch the World Cup 2026 on Peacock?
items.a: Peacock Premium starts at $7.99/month. It includes Telemundo's full World Cup catalogue in Spanish plus on-demand replays. Paying a single month covers the entire tournament window.

items.q: When does Spain play at the 2026 World Cup?
items.a: Spain is in Group H with Uruguay, Saudi Arabia and Cape Verde. The first match is on June 15 vs Cape Verde at Atlanta (Mercedes-Benz Stadium) at 12:00 EDT. The second is on June 22 vs Saudi Arabia in Atlanta at 12:00 EDT. The third and most awaited is June 26 vs Uruguay in Guadalajara (Estadio Akron) at 18:00 CST.

items.q: How do I watch the World Cup 2026 from abroad if I'm a US viewer?
items.a: Fox and Peacock subscribers can use their app inside the US. Outside the US, the cleanest path is a local data eSIM and the local broadcaster's platform, or rely on FIFA's free global stream on its DAZN partnership where available.

items.q: When is the World Cup 2026 final and where to watch it?
items.a: The final is on July 19, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, which FIFA renames «New York New Jersey Stadium» for the tournament. Free-to-air via Fox in English and Telemundo in Spanish, plus streaming on Tubi and Peacock.

items.q: How many matches are there at the World Cup 2026?
items.a: For the first time, 104 matches are played: 72 group-stage (12 groups × 6 matches), 16 round of 32, 8 round of 16, 4 quarter-finals, 2 semi-finals, the third-place match and the final.

disclaimer.body: Prices, plans and broadcast rights can change. Before subscribing, verify directly with the platform's official site. Links to Movistar+, DAZN, RTVE and others are public information, not affiliate links yet.

entradas.title: 2026 World Cup Tickets · How to buy, prices and sales phases
entradas.description: Complete guide to buying 2026 FIFA World Cup tickets: official prices by category, Phase 4 sales open until the final, hospitality packages and direct links to FIFA Tickets.
keywords: how to buy World Cup 2026 tickets
entradas.intro: How to buy, what the prices are and which sales phases remain open for the 104 matches of the first 48-team World Cup. Guide updated to April 2026.

securityNotice.strong: FIFA is the only official seller.
securityNotice.body: Any website offering tickets at face value outside of fifa.com/tickets is unauthorized resale or a scam. Your ticket can be canceled at the stadium gate.

phasesSection.kicker: When sales happened and what's left

p1.name: Phase 1, Visa Presale Draw
p1.desc: Random draw reserved for Visa cardholders.

p2.name: Phase 2, Early Ticket Draw
p2.desc: Second random draw before the group-stage draw was known.

p3.name: Phase 3, Random Selection Draw
p3.desc: Third draw, with groups already known. Specific match assignment per team.

p4.name: Phase 4, Last-Minute Sales
p4.desc: Continuous first-come, first-served sales (no draw). Real-time dynamic pricing based on demand. Availability for the 104 matches subject to inventory.

pricesSection.intro: Prices in US dollars, subject to FIFA's dynamic pricing: actual cost depends on the venue, the teams and demand at the moment of purchase.
pricesSection.footer: There's a $60 Supporter Tier available only to accredited members of the official Fan Clubs of participating teams. Final-match resale has reached $2 million per ticket on secondary markets.

groupCat3.notes: Most affordable category. Prices rise with demand.

groupCat1.notes: Best stadium zone. Upper cap varies by match.

hospitalitySection.intro: When affordable individual tickets are gone, official hospitality packages are the only way in. Sold on hospitality.fifa.com.

fifaPavilion.name: FIFA Pavilion
fifaPavilion.perks: Premium seat, shared lounge, fast-track entry.

championsClub.name: Champions Club
championsClub.perks: Menus designed by local chefs, cocktails, climate-controlled lounge.

pitchsideLounge.name: Pitchside Lounge
pitchsideLounge.perks: Almost field-side access, premium bar included.

suitesPrivate.name: Private suites
suitesPrivate.perks: Multi-match packages (incl. final) at MetLife and Azteca.

items.q: Where do you buy World Cup 2026 tickets?
items.a: Only on the official site fifa.com/tickets. FIFA is the sole authorized seller. Any other site offering tickets at face value is resale or, at worst, a scam.

items.q: Are tickets still available in April 2026?
items.a: Yes. Phase 4 (Last-Minute Sales) opened on April 1, 2026 and stays active until the July 19 final. Purchases are first-come, first-served and prices are dynamic: they can rise or drop in real time based on demand.

items.q: How much does a 2026 World Cup ticket cost?
items.a: The most affordable public tickets start at $120 (Cat. 3, group stage at lower-demand venues). The final costs from $1,490 in Cat. 3 up to $7,875 in Cat. 1. There is a $60 Supporter Tier but it is reserved for the official Fan Clubs of participating teams.

items.q: What is dynamic pricing?
items.a: For the first time at a World Cup, FIFA applies dynamic pricing: the cost of a ticket changes in real time based on demand, the teams playing and the venue. A ticket that opens a phase at $200 can end up costing $350 before that same phase closes.

items.q: Can I buy tickets on the resale market?
items.a: FIFA has an official resale platform inside fifa.com/tickets. It's the only legal and safe way to resell a ticket. Third-party sites (StubHub, Viagogo, etc.) are not authorized: tickets can be canceled at the stadium gate.

items.q: Do I need a visa to enter the US, Mexico or Canada with the ticket?
items.a: The match ticket does not exempt you from immigration requirements. Each country applies its own rules: ESTA for the US, FMM for Mexico and eTA for Canada. Processing can take weeks, so handle it as soon as you have the ticket.

items.q: When is the opening match and when is the final?
items.a: The opener is on June 11, 2026 at 1:00 PM Mexico time (Mexico vs South Africa) at Estadio Azteca. The final is on July 19, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey.

ctaFinal.h2: Buy them on the official site
ctaFinal.intro: This page is informational: we don't sell or resell tickets. For a safe transaction always use fifa.com/tickets or FIFA's official resale system.

howTo.name: How to buy 2026 World Cup tickets
howTo.description: Step-by-step guide to buying official 2026 World Cup tickets on fifa.com/tickets during sales phase 4.

steps.name: Create or sign in to your FIFA account
steps.text: Go to fifa.com/tickets and create an account with a verified email. Real name matching your ID document is required.

steps.name: Pick the match
steps.text: On the «Matches» tab, filter by date, city or team. Availability is subject to inventory and dynamic pricing.

steps.name: Select category and seat
steps.text: Choose between Cat. 1, 2 or 3. The Supporter option ($60) only appears if you are an accredited member of your team's Fan Club.

steps.name: Pay with Visa or accepted card
steps.text: Visa is the official partner. Other cards also work but may have currency restrictions.

steps.name: Get your digital ticket in your account
steps.text: Tickets are personal and digital (FIFA Mobile App). They cannot be printed or freely transferred.

fanZone.description: How to gear up your home for the 2026 World Cup: 4K TVs, soundbars, projectors and rituals. The screen, the sound and the people that turn the tournament into something more than an event.

sedes.intro: From the Estadio Azteca in Mexico City to MetLife Stadium in New York/New Jersey, plus Vancouver, Toronto, Atlanta, Dallas and ten more host cities. Each venue has its stadium, schedule, travel plan, hotel districts and on-the-ground tips.

sedes.title: World Cup 2026 host cities: all 16 venues, matches and travel guide
sedes.description: All 16 host cities of the 2026 FIFA World Cup across the USA, Mexico and Canada. Schedule by city, stadium, capacity, what to do, where to stay and how to get there.

calendario.title: 2026 World Cup schedule: 104 matches by stage, dates and kick-off times
calendario.description: Full 2026 World Cup schedule broken down by stage: groups, round of 32, round of 16, quarter-finals, semi-finals, third-place match and final. Dates, kick-off times and venues.
calendario.intro: Structure of the 2026 World Cup: the first edition with 48 teams introduces a new round of 32 between the group stage and the classic round of 16. The tournament runs for 39 days, from the opening match at the Estadio Azteca to the final at MetLife Stadium.
calendario.phaseGroupDesc: 72 matches across 12 groups (round-robin of 6 matches per group). The top 2 plus the 8 best third-placed sides go through, 32 teams in total.
calendario.phaseR32Desc: 16 single-leg knockouts never seen before at a World Cup: the first cut-off, the winner advances to the round of 16. A novelty of the 48-team format.
calendario.phaseR16Desc: 8 single-leg knockouts. By now the tournament's heavyweights are in. Winners head to the quarter-finals.
calendario.phaseQFDesc: 4 knockout ties. After a rest day, the tournament's final sprint kicks off.
calendario.phaseSFDesc: 2 matches at two different venues. The winners play the final, the losers contest the third-place match.
calendario.phase3PDesc: The consolation match for the eliminated semi-finalists.
calendario.phaseFinalDesc: 19 July at MetLife Stadium (New York/New Jersey). The champions lift the trophy.
calendario.fixturesPending: Match-ups will be confirmed once the previous stage ends.

grupos.title: 2026 World Cup groups: A-L with the 48 teams, host cities and schedule
grupos.description: The 12 groups of the 2026 World Cup (A-L) with the 48 teams, flags, host cities for each match and full group-stage schedule.
grupos.intro: Final draw held on 5 December 2025 at the Kennedy Center in Washington D.C. The top 2 from each group plus the 8 best third-placed sides go through, 32 teams in total to a brand-new round of 32 never seen before at a World Cup.
grupos.qualifyKicker: How teams qualify for the round of 32
grupos.qualifyBody: Each group plays 6 matches in a round-robin (everyone vs everyone). The top 2 from each group go through (24 teams) plus the 8 best third-placed sides across the tournament (out of 12 candidates). Total: 32 teams to a round of 32 played from 28 June to 3 July.

wc2026Hub.intro: 48 nations. 104 matches. 16 cities. 3 countries. The first World Cup of the new format.

records.topScorersIntro: The all-time scoring ranking at FIFA World Cups. Klose leads with 16, Ronaldo is second with 15, Müller has 14. Messi and Mbappé are the active players who have climbed the most.
records.yellowsIntro: The flip side of fair play. Javier Mascherano leads the all-time list with seven yellows, several of them in finals and semi-finals. A yellow at a World Cup is always a decision: take a foul to avoid losing your opponent.
records.yellowsDisclaimer: Data extracted from matches with minute-by-minute events recorded in our database. Coverage is complete from the 1994 World Cup; earlier tournaments may have unrecorded cards if the official report was sparse.
records.redsIntro: A red card at a World Cup is almost always the end of a tournament. Only a handful of players have been sent off in different editions, with Rigobert Song the most well-known case. Here are all of them.
records.redsDisclaimer: Full coverage from the 1994 World Cup. Before that edition, sendings-off are recorded only when the official report is available: players such as Rigobert Song (1994, 1998) and Zinedine Zidane (1998, 2006), with two reds each, are the known historic ceiling.
records.minutesIntro: Beyond the squad lists, the actual minutes on the pitch. Paolo Maldini held the all-time record for two decades with 2,217 minutes in 23 matches (1990-2002), until Lionel Messi surpassed him at Qatar 2022. Lothar Matthäus, in turn, still holds the matches-played record with 25 (1982-1998), now also matched by Messi with 26.
records.minutesDisclaimer: Absolute record: Lionel Messi (Argentina) overtook Paolo Maldini's 2,217 minutes in the Qatar 2022 final. Our minute-by-minute coverage is complete from 1994; earlier tournaments may underestimate minutes when the official report did not detail substitution times.
records.wcsPlayedIntro: Only six players in history have played in five World Cups: Antonio Carbajal (Mexico, 1950-1966), Lothar Matthäus (Germany, 1982-1998), Rafael Márquez (Mexico, 2002-2018), Andrés Guardado (Mexico, 2006-2022), Lionel Messi (Argentina, 2006-2022) and Cristiano Ronaldo (Portugal, 2006-2022). Messi and CR7 could add a sixth in 2026.
records.wcsPlayedDisclaimer: Absolute record: 6 players with 5 World Cups (list above). In matches the ranking is led by Messi with 26, followed by Matthäus with 25 and CR7 with 22.
records.ownGoalsIntro: The most expensive own goal in World Cup history was scored by Andrés Escobar against the United States in 1994 (Colombia knocked out in the group stage). Ten days later, Escobar was murdered in Medellín. Here are all the official own goals at the tournament since 1930.
records.ownGoalsDisclaimer: Trivia: no player has scored more than one own goal in World Cup history. There are players who scored at both ends in the same tournament (Ernie Brandts in 1978, Ruud Krol in 1974, Marcelo in 2018). Russia 2018 broke the single-edition record with 12 own goals; the cumulative total from 1930 to 2022 exceeds 50.
records.teamCardsIntro: Brazil leads the all-time sendings-off record (10 reds across 22 World Cups played). Argentina (9) and Uruguay (8) complete the South American podium. Every match counts: the total is cumulative, not per edition.
records.teamCardsDisclaimer: The ranking reflects the cumulative total, not relative discipline. Brazil, Argentina and Uruguay sit at the top in part because they have the most appearances (22, 18 and 14 World Cups respectively). Minute-by-minute card coverage is complete from 1994; in earlier tournaments the records may underestimate light bookings not captured in the official report.

mascotas.title: 2026 World Cup mascots: Maple, Zayu and Clutch · Canada, Mexico and USA
mascotas.description: The three official mascots of the 2026 FIFA World Cup represent the host countries: Maple (moose, Canada), Zayu (jaguar, Mexico) and Clutch (bald eagle, USA). History, symbolism and FIFA design.
mascotas.intro: For the first time in history, the World Cup has three official mascots — one per host country. A moose, a jaguar and a bald eagle represent Canada, Mexico and the United States in the first 48-team tournament. FIFA design unveiled in September 2025.

meta.title: LEGO World Cup 2026: the 10 official sets with Messi, Ronaldo, Mbappé and Vinicius
meta.description: Complete catalog of the LEGO FIFA World Cup 2026 collection: 10 official sets featuring Messi (43011, 43015, 43018), Cristiano Ronaldo (43012, 43016), Mbappé (43013), Vinícius Jr (43027), the 1:1 Trophy (43020 · 2,842 pieces), the official emblem (43032) and the USA jersey (43033). Prices, set numbers, Amazon ASINs, release dates and where to buy.

legoMundial2026.intro: The official LEGO × FIFA World Cup 2026 collection brings 10 sets with the four big ambassadors: Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo, Mbappé and Vinícius Jr. Two premium sets at $199.99: the 1:1 World Cup Trophy (43020, 2,842 pieces) already on pre-order since March, and the Messi Celebration (43018) arriving June 1. The rest launches May 1. Prices from $24.99.
legoMundial2026.ctaLego: See the full collection at LEGO.com
legoMundial2026.heroImageAlt: Official LEGO logo. The brand has launched ten sets dedicated to the 2026 World Cup featuring Messi, Ronaldo, Mbappé and Vinicius Jr
legoMundial2026.affiliateNotice: Amazon Spain links with nuus-21 affiliation. ASINs are global (same on .com/.es/.de) but availability may vary; if Amazon.es is out of stock, you'll be redirected to the product search. For LEGO.com exclusives (emblem 43032 and USA jersey 43033) the button goes straight to the official store.

s43020.title: FIFA World Cup · Official Trophy
s43020.subtitle: 1:1 trophy replica · 2,842 pieces
s43020.description: 1:1 replica of the FIFA World Cup Trophy at 36 cm tall and 2,842 pieces. Hidden compartment in the upper sphere with the 2026 World Cup logo inside. Exclusive LEGO minifigure with mini-trophy and a plaque listing every champion since 1974. First time LEGO uses this many real-gold pieces (lacquered and molded) in a single set.

s43018.title: Lionel Messi · Celebration
s43018.subtitle: Premium wall display set · 14+
s43018.description: The collection's second flagship: Messi Celebration in wall-display format. Recreates the moment on December 18, 2022 when Messi lifted the World Cup in Doha. Aimed at AFOL builders and 2022 World Cup fans. Arrives 10 days before the opening match at Estadio Azteca.

s43015.title: Lionel Messi · Soccer Legend
s43015.subtitle: Buildable figure · 958 pieces
s43015.description: Buildable Messi figure in a victory pose pointing skyward (after scoring) or in a dynamic dribble pose. 958 pieces. Argentine albiceleste base, signature plaque and CR-style stats. Companion piece to the 43016 (Cristiano).

s43016.title: Cristiano Ronaldo · Soccer Legend
s43016.subtitle: Buildable figure · 854 pieces
s43016.description: Buildable Cristiano figure measuring 25 × 23 × 13 cm with two build options: the «Siuuu» celebration pose or a bicycle kick. 854 pieces. Co-molded face, number 7, a brand-new ball element, large CR7 backdrop and signature plaque. The first time Cristiano and Messi officially share LEGO shelf space.

s43011.title: Lionel Messi · Soccer Highlights
s43011.subtitle: Diorama · 500 pieces
s43011.description: Scene with a base shaped like Messi's initial, the Argentine albiceleste colors, oversized number 10, goal net and a mini build of career milestones. Messi minifigure in a goal-scoring pose plus stats plaque. Hidden Easter eggs throughout. 500 pieces, 13 cm tall × 24 cm wide.

s43012.title: Cristiano Ronaldo · Soccer Highlights
s43012.subtitle: Diorama · Siuuu scene
s43012.description: «Siuuu» scene with Cristiano celebrating a goal. Letter-shaped base in Portuguese colors. LEGO minifigure with the number 7 on the chest and an ID plaque with stats.

s43013.title: Kylian Mbappé · Soccer Highlights
s43013.subtitle: Diorama · 490 pieces
s43013.description: Scene of Mbappé in an explosive run wearing France's number 10. Base shaped like Mbappé's initial, blue Les Bleus colors and a buildable number 10. LEGO minifigure in goal-scoring pose with stats and signature plaque. 490 pieces.

s43027.title: Vinícius Jr · Soccer Highlights
s43027.subtitle: Diorama · 510 pieces
s43027.description: Vini Jr scene with a V-shaped base, Brazil's green-and-yellow colors and the number 7. LEGO minifigure with his trademark haircut, goal-scoring pose and a stats and signature plaque. Easter eggs from his career. 510 pieces.

s43032.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 · Official Emblem
s43032.subtitle: Official logo display
s43032.description: Semi-3D rendering of the World Cup Trophy on a colorful flat panel with the «26» from the official FIFA logo. 298 pieces. Dedicated display base and a wall-hanging hook. LEGO.com / LEGO Store exclusive.

s43033.title: U.S. Soccer National Team Jersey
s43033.subtitle: USA jersey in LEGO
s43033.description: Wall-display rendering of the United States home jersey for the 2026 World Cup (host nation). 167 pieces. LEGO.com / LEGO Store exclusive. Aimed at the U.S. market but collectible worldwide.

highlights.label: Football Highlights
highlights.detail: Dioramas for ages 10+ with minifigure. Messi, Cristiano, Mbappé, Vinicius.

legend.label: Football Legend
legend.detail: Buildable figures, 854-958 pieces. Messi and Cristiano.

merch.label: Merchandise
merch.detail: Emblem (43032) and USA jersey (43033). LEGO.com exclusives.

items.q: How many LEGO World Cup 2026 sets are there?
items.a: Ten official sets in the LEGO Editions FIFA World Cup 2026 collection. They split into four categories: two «Football Legend» (Messi 43015 and Cristiano 43016, $79.99), four «Football Highlights» (Messi 43011, Cristiano 43012, Mbappé 43013, Vinicius 43027 at $29.99), two premium display sets (official Trophy 43020 and Messi Celebration 43018 at $199.99) and two merchandise pieces (emblem 43032 and USA jersey 43033 at $24.99).

items.q: When do the 2026 World Cup LEGO sets launch?
items.a: The Trophy 43020 opened pre-orders in March 2026. The Football Legend (43015, 43016), Football Highlights (43011, 43012, 43013, 43027), emblem (43032) and USA jersey (43033) arrive May 1. The Messi Celebration (43018) wall display lands June 1. Global rollout: USA, Spain, UK and Germany first; LATAM (Mexico, Argentina, Brazil) one to two weeks later.

items.q: How much does the Messi LEGO set cost?
items.a: Messi has three sets in the collection: «Football Highlights» 43011 at $29.99 (500 pieces), «Football Legend» 43015 at $79.99 (958 pieces, buildable figure) and «Messi Celebration» 43018 at $199.99 (premium wall display, June 1). Cristiano Ronaldo matches the first two: 43012 ($29.99) and 43016 ($79.99, 854 pieces).

items.q: Where can I buy the 2026 World Cup LEGO sets?
items.a: LEGO.com is the main official channel, with guaranteed stock and the exclusives (emblem 43032 and USA jersey 43033 ONLY here). Amazon stocks the rest (we link them above with nuus-21 affiliation). FNAC, El Corte Inglés and Toys'R'Us receive stock between May 5 and 15. In Mexico: Liverpool, Sears, Costco. In Brazil: Ri Happy, Lojas Americanas.

items.q: Which is the most expensive and the cheapest 2026 World Cup LEGO?
items.a: The most expensive are the official Trophy 43020 (2,842 pieces) and Messi Celebration 43018, both at $199.99 / €179.99. The most affordable are the FIFA World Cup 2026 Official Emblem (43032, 298 pieces) and the USA jersey (43033, 167 pieces), both at $24.99 and LEGO.com exclusives. Mid-range are the Messi and Cristiano Football Legend ($79.99) and the Football Highlights ($29.99).

items.q: How many pieces does the LEGO Trophy 43020 have?
items.a: 2,842 pieces. It's the first time in LEGO history that a single set uses this many real-gold pieces (lacquered and molded). It stands 36 cm tall, recommended for ages 12+. Includes a hidden compartment in the upper sphere with the 2026 World Cup logo inside and a plaque listing every champion since 1974. Amazon ASIN: B0FMYYGFQF.

items.q: Which players appear in LEGO World Cup 2026?
items.a: The four ambassadors of the collection are Lionel Messi (Argentina, 3 sets: 43011, 43015, 43018), Cristiano Ronaldo (Portugal, 2 sets: 43012, 43016), Kylian Mbappé (France, set 43013) and Vinícius Jr (Brazil, set 43027).

items.q: Is there a LEGO set for the Spanish national team?
items.a: Not in the first wave. This first batch only includes one national-team set: the USA (host, set 43033). LEGO could expand the line with more top-seeded teams depending on sales as the tournament unfolds. It's the usual LEGO Editions playbook: launch a first wave, measure sales, expand.

ctaBlock.intro: Guaranteed stock for the full catalog and the Messi Celebration exclusive. Official LEGO shipping with tracking.

meta.title: World Cup 2026 favorites: odds, ranking and analysis of the 12 contenders
meta.description: Who will win the 2026 World Cup: ranking of the 12 favorites by bookmakers (Bet365, William Hill, Pinnacle), squad analysis, group breakdown and a realistic forecast. Argentina, France, Brazil, Spain, England and the outsiders.

favoritosGanarMundial.intro: Argentina opens as favorite at 5/1 across the major bookmakers. France, Brazil, Spain and England round out the top-5. Here are the 12 serious title contenders, with their World Cup group, star player, key group-stage match and an honest read on strengths and risks.

ranking.intro: Reference odds compiled from Bet365, William Hill, Pinnacle and Stake in May 2026. Odds update weekly as the tournament approaches and pre-World Cup friendlies feed information to the traders. Ranking sorted by lowest odds (highest implied probability).

ARG.fortaleza: Reigning champion with the Qatar 2022 core reinforced by a fully consolidated Julián Álvarez and Mac Allister at his best.
ARG.riesgo: Messi turns 39 during the tournament. If he doesn't deliver like in Qatar, the team loses its main card on the moments that matter.
ARG.estrella: Lionel Messi · possible World Cup farewell

FRA.fortaleza: Mbappé arrives as PSG captain with 2 Champions League titles and 5 French league titles. Tchouaméni and Camavinga lock down the midfield.
FRA.riesgo: The post-Varane defense is the weakest in three decades. Any opponent with pace up top can exploit the left side.
FRA.estrella: Kylian Mbappé · captain and Qatar 2022 Golden Boot

BRA.fortaleza: The Vinicius Jr / Rodrygo / Endrick / Raphinha generation is the deepest attacking pool since Ronaldo Naz / Ronaldinho in 2002.
BRA.riesgo: Without a 100% Casemiro, the double pivot is brittle. Young center-backs haven't been through a major tournament with the responsibility of the Silva era.
BRA.estrella: Vinícius Jr · captain and 2024 Ballon d'Or contender

ESP.fortaleza: Euro 2024 champion with Lamine Yamal in full flight, Pedri injury-free and Ballon d'Or Rodri back in form.
ESP.riesgo: Heat and altitude in Guadalajara for the Uruguay match (J3) is the only variable Spain hasn't been able to simulate.
ESP.estrella: Lamine Yamal · 19 years old, Champions and Euro on his shoulders

ENG.fortaleza: Bellingham is the world's best #10 and Saka, Foden and Kane form England's most complete squad since 1990.
ENG.riesgo: The recent record of penalty shootout exits still weighs heavily. Tuchel has to break the mental wall.
ENG.estrella: Jude Bellingham · captain and 2024 La Liga winner with Real Madrid

GER.fortaleza: Moral host of Euro 2024 (home semifinalist). Wirtz and Musiala bring creativity unseen in 20 years.
GER.riesgo: Rebuilding phase after the Qatar 2022 disaster. The new generation hasn't lifted an international tournament yet.
GER.estrella: Florian Wirtz · Bundesliga MVP and the new galáctico
GER.partidoClave: Jun 21 · Germany-Ivory Coast · BMO Field · Toronto

POR.fortaleza: Cristiano Ronaldo's last World Cup chance plus the rise of Bernardo Silva, Bruno Fernandes and the Leão / Conceição duo.
POR.riesgo: Coexistence between the historic CR7 ego and the new leaders. Any locker-room friction sinks the project.
POR.estrella: Cristiano Ronaldo · fifth and confirmed final World Cup

NED.fortaleza: The 2010 generation reframed with Frenkie de Jong at his best, Gakpo and Simons leading the attack.
NED.riesgo: Post-Van Dijk central defense is vulnerable. A carbon copy of the historic pattern: top-tier attack, irregular defense.
NED.estrella: Cody Gakpo · Champions League finalist and Liverpool starter

CRO.fortaleza: Modrić is still the tactical brain. The generational succession Sosa / Petković has improved through 2025-26.
CRO.riesgo: The age of the historic spine. If Modrić can't go 90 minutes, the team drops 30% in attacking output.
CRO.estrella: Luka Modrić · his last World Cup?

BEL.fortaleza: Last generational shot for De Bruyne / Lukaku / Hazard. Doku adds the youthful explosiveness they lacked in Qatar.
BEL.riesgo: The disconnect between veterans and young players in the 2025 qualifiers was obvious. Tedesco hasn't gelled the group.
BEL.estrella: Kevin De Bruyne · brain and generational captain
BEL.partidoClave: Group G is accessible if they top it

USA.fortaleza: Hosts with home-field advantage in a favorable bracket. Pulisic, Reyna and McKennie play at top European clubs.
USA.riesgo: No World Cup tradition for handling home pressure. FIFA may push them along, but they arrive at the World Cup without having won anything serious.
USA.estrella: Christian Pulisic · captain and Milan starter
USA.partidoClave: Jun 12 · USA-Paraguay · SoFi · Los Angeles · co-opening match

MEX.fortaleza: Opening with the inaugural match at Estadio Azteca, a unique scenario in their World Cup history. Edson Álvarez and Hugo Sánchez (assistant) are the emotional spine.
MEX.riesgo: Same historic pattern: strong group stage, knocked out in round of 16. For 2026 they have to break «the curse of the 5th match».
MEX.estrella: Edson Álvarez · captain and West Ham starter
MEX.partidoClave: Jun 11 · Mexico-South Africa · Estadio Azteca · CDMX · opening match

items.label: Hypothetical final
items.text: Argentina-France, an exact rerun of Qatar 2022. Favorable bracket for both sides until quarter-finals, where they'd meet Brazil and Spain respectively. Probability: 25-30%.

items.label: Most likely surprise
items.text: Morocco reaching beyond the quarter-finals. They have a favorable bracket on Argentina's side (round-of-16 cross against the second-place finisher of an accessible group) and the 2022 generation is largely intact.

items.label: Team to avoid
items.text: England. They're the 5th favorite but their recent record in finals tournaments (3 consecutive Euros, none won with a squad similar to the current one) hasn't shifted. Knockout-round pressure could break them again.

items.label: The host phenomenon
items.text: USA and Mexico arrive with home-field advantage but their World Cup history doesn't back it up. Mexico has been knocked out in the round of 16 in 7 of the last 8 World Cups. The USA reached at most the quarter-finals in 2002. Hard to see them breaking that ceiling in 2026 even at home.

items.q: Who is favorite to win the 2026 World Cup?
items.a: Argentina is the main favorite according to bookmakers (5/1 at Bet365 and William Hill, May 2026), followed by France (6/1), Brazil (7/1) and Spain (8/1). England rounds out the top-5 at 9/1. These five teams account for 70% of the money wagered on the World Cup winner.

items.q: Why is Argentina the favorite?
items.a: For three reasons: they are the reigning champion and keep the Qatar 2022 core, they have the consolidated Messi-Julián Álvarez double card, and a group (J: Algeria, Austria, Jordan) considered the most accessible among UEFA or CONMEBOL top-seeded groups. Everything points to a favorable bracket through to the quarter-finals.

items.q: When does each favorite kick off?
items.a: Mexico opens the 2026 World Cup on June 11 at Estadio Azteca against South Africa. The USA debuts on June 12. Brazil plays on the 13th against Morocco at MetLife. Germany and the Netherlands on the 14th. Spain on the 15th in Atlanta. France and Argentina on the 16th. England on the 17th. The group stage ends on June 27.

items.q: Are bookmaker odds reliable as a ranking?
items.a: They reflect the money wagered, not necessarily the real probability. They are useful as an expectation thermometer: odds <8/1 mean teams with real title chances; 10-30/1 are competitive teams with a semis ceiling; >30/1 are outsiders that need a favorable bracket and a perfect tournament.

items.q: Is this the first time the World Cup is played in 3 countries?
items.a: Yes. The 2026 World Cup is the first ever hosted by three countries simultaneously (USA, Mexico, Canada). It's also the first with 48 teams (up from 32 since 1998), introduces a new round of 32 and lasts 39 days instead of the usual 32. The final will be played on July 19 at MetLife Stadium, New York/New Jersey.

items.q: Who is the dark horse of the 2026 World Cup?
items.a: Morocco is the logical pick after their Qatar 2022 semifinal (40/1 odds). Senegal looks like a round-of-16 possibility thanks to their accessible group (France + Iraq + Norway). In CONMEBOL, Uruguay with the Núñez-Valverde-Pellistri generation can spring a surprise. And Cape Verde, an absolute debutant, arrives with all the sentimental factor.

favoritosGanarMundial.articleHeadline: World Cup 2026 favorites: ranking, odds and analysis of the 12 contenders
favoritosGanarMundial.articleDescription: Who will win the 2026 World Cup according to bookmakers and editorial analysis: Argentina, France, Brazil, Spain, England and outsiders.

meta.title: World Cup 2026 squads: provisional list May 11, final June 1
meta.description: When the 48 teams publish their World Cup 2026 squads. FIFA sets the preliminary list on May 11 and the final list on June 1. 26 players per team, 3 mandatory goalkeepers.
keywords: when are world cup squads announced

convocatorias2026.intro: The 48 teams of the 2026 World Cup hand FIFA their provisional list on May 11 and the final one on June 1. Here's the official calendar, the squad size, the injury-replacement rules and when each federation is expected to announce its squad publicly.

items.title: Provisional list (release list)
items.detail: Every federation submits a list of 35 to 55 players to FIFA. It is internal: FIFA does NOT publish it. National-team coaches usually announce their preliminary list at a press conference in the days leading up.

items.title: Final list
items.detail: Deadline to submit the final squad to FIFA: between 23 and 26 players, with 3 mandatory goalkeepers. Most federations release this list on the same day they submit it.

items.title: Opening match · Estadio Azteca
items.detail: Mexico vs South Africa kicks off the tournament. From this point on, injury replacements are only allowed up to 24 hours before each team's FIRST match, certified by the national-team doctor and a FIFA doctor.

squad.intro: Between 23 and 26 total players per squad, with a mandatory minimum of 3 goalkeepers. It's the same cap that was applied for the first time at Qatar 2022. Up through Russia 2018, teams could bring at most 23 players. The expansion was introduced during the pandemic to allow more rotation, and FIFA keeps it for 2026 with a longer, more demanding tournament (the new round of 32 adds an extra match).

notes.2022: Pandemic: FIFA raised the cap to 26 already in Qatar.
notes.2026: The 26-player cap stays for the 48-team format.

replacements.allowedBody: Up to 24 hours before the team's first match, FIFA allows replacing a called-up player due to injury or illness. The team doctor certifies the case and a FIFA medical officer validates it. It happens at every World Cup: the last loan-back from Bayern, Madrid or Manchester leaves someone out and the substitute on the provisional list comes in.
replacements.forbiddenBody: Once the team's first match is played, FIFA does NOT allow substitutions. Even if a player gets injured on matchday one, the team finishes the tournament with the remaining fit players. That was Italy with Cabrini in 1990, Argentina with Caniggia and many others: once you're in, you're in until the end.

federations.intro: Estimated date and venue for the public squad announcement, based on each federation's pattern at previous World Cups. Numbers will be confirmed as each team makes them official.

ESP.name: Spain
ESP.expected: First week of May (Las Rozas, RFEF)

POR.name: Portugal
POR.expected: First half of May (Cidade do Futebol, FPF)

ENG.name: England
ENG.expected: First half of May (St. George's Park, FA)

NED.name: Netherlands
NED.expected: First half of May (KNVB Campus, KNVB)

items.q: When do teams publish their World Cup 2026 squad?
items.a: FIFA sets two deadlines: on May 11, 2026 federations submit a provisional list of 35 to 55 players (which FIFA does not publish), and on June 1, 2026 they hand in the final 23-to-26-player squad. Most teams announce both at a press conference on the same day they submit it to FIFA or in the days leading up.

items.q: How many players go per team to the 2026 World Cup?
items.a: Between 23 and 26 players, with 3 mandatory goalkeepers. It's the same format used at Qatar 2022. At previous World Cups through 2018 the cap was 23 players.

items.q: When will Spain announce the World Cup 2026 squad?
items.a: The RFEF traditionally publishes its provisional list at a press conference in Las Rozas in early May. The exact date is not confirmed, but it's expected between May 5 and 11, 2026, ahead of the FIFA deadline. The final squad will be known between May 25 and June 1.

items.q: What happens if a player gets injured after the squad is named?
items.a: Up to 24 hours before the team's first match, FIFA allows replacing him if the team doctor and a FIFA doctor certify the injury. Once the first match is played, no substitutions are permitted: the team finishes the tournament with the remaining fit players.

items.q: Why is the squad 26 players and not 23 like before?
items.a: FIFA expanded the cap to 26 at Qatar 2022 as a pandemic exception and given the closeness to the European season. For 2026 the figure stays: with 48 teams and a more demanding calendar (round of 32 added), it makes sense to allow more rotation.

items.q: Can the squad change between June 1 and June 11?
items.a: Yes, but only for confirmed injury or illness. Each change requires a certificate from the national-team doctor and validation from the FIFA medical officer. There are no free substitutions.

items.q: How many goalkeepers must they bring?
items.a: Three mandatory goalkeepers within the 23-26 range. If a team brings 26 players, it'll have 3 goalkeepers and 23 outfield players. If it brings 23, it'll have 3 goalkeepers and 20 outfield.

items.q: When are the shirt numbers published?
items.a: Together with the final list on June 1. FIFA requires each player to wear a fixed number from 1 to 26 throughout the tournament, and the AFA, the RFEF and other federations usually reveal them on the same day as the official announcement.

faq.sourceNote: Source: «Regulations, FIFA World Cup 26» (PDF, March 19, 2026) · Wikipedia «2026 FIFA World Cup squads». Last verified: April 2026.
